title: Gently Simmered Mexican Chicken Stew
description: A nutty and shaggy Veracruzan-inspired dish featuring coconut cream, perfect for a brunch mood, this paleo-friendly stew is slow-cooked to perfection. It combines the flavors of Mexico with the creaminess of coconut.
instructions: In a large Dutch oven, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the chicken to the pot and cook until it is browned on all sides.
Add the chili powder, cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the diced tomatoes and coconut cream, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 30 minutes.
Stir the stew and continue to cook, covered, for an additional 15 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the sauce has thickened.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro or scallions, if desired.
